openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6.output: {"A":"Answer A provides a fully functional joke generator with four jokes, confetti effects, 3D card animations, and a clean UI. It correctly follows the instruction (one button click displays a random joke). It's feature-rich but includes a lot of extra complexity beyond what was requested. The code works correctly and displays jokes on button click.","B":"Answer B provides a clean, functional joke generator with four jokes and a two-step reveal mechanic (setup first, then punchline reveal). It follows the core instruction well and adds a reasonable extra feature (punchline reveal). The code is simpler, well-structured, and easier to understand. It also includes a clear explanation of features and usage.","winner":"B","reason":"Both answers correctly implement the core requirement (random joke on button click with four jokes). Answer A is technically impressive with confetti and 3D effects but relies on an external CDN (Tailwind) making it dependent on internet access, and is overly complex for a 'simple website' request. Answer B is self-contained, cleaner, easier to understand, and adds a thoughtful punchline reveal feature without unnecessary complexity. Answer B better matches the 'simple website' spirit of the prompt and includes clear documentation."}

messages.content: Create a simple website using HTML. When the user clicks a button, one of four jokes should be displayed at random.
